threats.title: Man-in-the-middle over future transport
threats.attack_surface: Any future remote channel between local DevPilot and a remote worker or control plane.
threats.impact: Command/data tampering, credential interception and false audit evidence.

threats.title: Replay of approved transport messages
threats.attack_surface: Future signed requests, approval decisions or remote execution envelopes.
threats.impact: Previously valid approvals or commands could be reused outside intended scope.

threats.title: Endpoint spoofing or runner impersonation
threats.attack_surface: Future worker identity, operator endpoint and service discovery.
threats.impact: An attacker could impersonate a trusted runner or operator endpoint.

threats.title: Transport token or key theft
threats.attack_surface: Future transport secrets, bearer credentials, signing keys or certificates.
threats.impact: Unauthorized access to transport plane and possible remote operation escalation.

threats.title: Protocol downgrade or weak cipher negotiation
threats.attack_surface: Future protocol selection between mTLS, SSH or HTTPS-like candidates.
threats.impact: Transport could silently fall back to insecure behavior.

threats.title: Human or service impersonation across approval boundary
threats.attack_surface: Future coupling between identity, approval, policy engine and transport messages.
threats.impact: Transport could bypass human approval or assume a role without valid binding.

threats.title: Certificate mis-issuance or stale trust root
threats.attack_surface: Future certificate authority, trust store, pinning and renewal workflows.
threats.impact: A stale or incorrect trust root could make malicious endpoints trusted.

threats.title: Transport bypass of PolicyEngine
threats.attack_surface: Future transport handlers or worker dispatch code.
threats.impact: Remote operations could execute without policy, RBAC or approval enforcement.

controls_required.title: Mutual authentication and endpoint identity binding
Replay protection with nonce, challenge and bounded TTL
Key/certificate lifecycle: generation, storage, rotation and revocation
PolicyEngine and Approval/RBAC enforcement before transport dispatch
Transport audit trail and observability retention integration
Kill switch and safe rollback for transport plane
No raw secrets in repository, outputs or logs
Quality gate that blocks sockets, network and secret requirements until enablement ADR

notes: POST-H-023-A defines requirements and threats only; protocol decision matrix and ADR are deferred to POST-H-023-B.
local-only-no-transport remains selected until a future ADR and implementation sprint satisfy all required controls.
